Optimization loops
The bottleneck

Revenue stalls where the work is manual.

Most founder-led teams don't have a demand problem. They have a throughput problem — leads leak, follow-up slips, and fulfillment can't scale past the founder's calendar.

🕳️

Leads leak

Inbound and outbound interest arrives faster than anyone can qualify, enrich, and route it. The pipeline never fills evenly.

⏳

Follow-up slips

The deal that needed a second touch at hour 24 gets it at day 9 — if at all. Speed-to-lead quietly caps your close rate.

🔗

Fulfillment chokes

Onboarding and delivery still run through the founder. Every new client adds drag instead of compounding momentum.

The operating system

Four agent layers. One control plane.

Each layer runs autonomously and reports into a single founder-controlled control plane. Nothing ships past a gate you didn't set.

01

Capture Layer

Lead generation, enrichment, and attribution across a multi-channel swarm — email, LinkedIn, X, and referral.

Demand
02

Decision Layer

Qualification scoring and routing so every contact gets the right next action — book, nurture, or disqualify.

Qualify
03

Conversion Layer

Follow-up automation and booking that closes the speed-to-lead gap and keeps pipeline moving without manual chase.

Convert
04

Fulfillment Layer

Onboarding automation and delivery QA so new clients compound momentum instead of adding founder drag.

Deliver
🛡️

Founder control preserved. Auditable logs and escalation thresholds keep strategy and mission decisions under operator authority — automation handles the execution, not the judgment.

30-day rollout

From kickoff to compounding in four weeks.

Week 1

Foundation

ICP definition, offer architecture, and full instrumentation so every signal is measured from day one.

Week 2

Lead ops

Lead operations workflow, CRM sync, and conversion scripts wired into the capture and decision layers.

Week 3

Delivery

Onboarding automation and a delivery scorecard so fulfillment scales without the founder in the loop.

Week 4

Iteration

ROI iteration and playbook optimization — the loop that keeps compounding after the sprint ends.

Book a strategy call

Tell us where revenue leaks.

Qualified operators get a 25-minute architecture session and a phased build plan mapped to your stack and revenue target.

  • Founder-led services, agencies & operators with active delivery
  • Targeting $10k–$100k of monthly revenue lift

$

✓

Request received.

We'll review your build request and follow up within one business day to schedule your 25-minute architecture session.

Questions

What founders ask first.

Is this fully autonomous?

Operations can be highly autonomous — capture, qualification, follow-up, and fulfillment run on agents. Strategy and mission decisions remain founder-controlled, with escalation thresholds and auditable logs at every gate.

Can this run with my existing tools?

Yes. The approach is integration-first — we wire the OS into your current CRM and stack. We only recommend replacing a tool when it's an active bottleneck, not by default.

How fast do I see something working?

Time to first signal is 48–96 hours. The full four-layer rollout runs over 30 days, with weekly optimization loops continuing after the sprint.

Who is this for?

Founder-led services, agencies, and operators with active delivery, targeting $10k–$100k of monthly revenue lift, ready to begin a first sprint within 7 days of booking.

What stays under my control?

You. Mission, offer, pricing, and any decision above your escalation threshold stay with you. Agents handle execution, not judgment — and every action is logged and reviewable.

Get started

Put the execution on autopilot.

Keep the part that needs you — the strategy, the relationships, the mission. Hand the rest to an operating system that never sleeps.

Book Strategy Call Run the ROI numbers

Autonomous revenue operations under full founder control. Your business runs itself — you run your business.
